item.title: Historic cattle shortages push US beef prices to record highs
item.description: High feed costs and drought have thinned herds to the lowest levels in 60 years as protein-hungry consumers drive demand

item.title: Crypto treasuries turn to risky equity in drive to raise cash
item.description: Companies that stock up on crypto assets are following Michael Saylor’s Strategy into so-called digital credit

item.title: Why does Castlelake want to buy easyJet?
item.description: Budget carrier has large fleet and expensive airport slots, while its share price has been hit by Iran crisis

item.title: Dotcom bubble IPOs
item.description: Lessons for boomtime investing

item.title: Arsenal’s dance with history
item.description: Also in today’s newsletter: how the Knicks built a championship contender

item.title: Ferrari’s new EV sparks Italian angst over Agnelli legacy
item.description: Critics say John Elkann has detached the family empire from its home country

item.title: Toshifumi Suzuki, convenience store king, 1932-2026
item.description: At the helm of the Japanese 7-Eleven, he revolutionised the country’s retail industry

item.title: Wealth tax fears reignited by UK leadership uncertainty
item.description: Contenders Streeting and Burnham have both hinted at tax reforms

item.title: Top UK building societies plot bids for digital bank Atom
item.description: Fintech’s owners hope the business will fetch about $600mn but industry executives say final offers could be lower

item.title: JPMorgan chases retail banking’s golden fleece
item.description: US bank’s international expansion is gaining some traction

item.title: London Stadium back in spotlight after West Ham’s Premier League exit
item.description: East London club will pay less rent for venue that has struggled to satisfy public since 2012 Olympics

item.title: Your wine collection may be worth less than you think
item.description: There are three main factors when it comes to valuing a fine wine

item.title: UK military looks at allowing lethal strikes without human approval
item.description: Officials push for machines to make autonomous decisions on targets in exceptional circumstances

item.title: Should AI steal your job?
item.description: The real question is not what the technology can do but what it ought to do. Sarah O’Connor on the people fighting for the future of work

item.title: How the Enhanced Games tripped over its own shoelaces
item.description: A business once valued at $1.2bn is now worth just $350mn

item.title: China’s coal heartlands reel from worst disaster in a decade
item.description: Deaths of 82 miners recall era of accidents many hoped was in the past

item.title: Private credit firm Castlelake explores bid for easyJet
item.description: Takeover of budget airline would lead to another UK-listed company taken off the stock market

item.title: Durex sales hit by China’s condom crackdown
item.description: Wide-ranging measures to boost the country’s birth rate are denting demand for China’s leading contraceptive brand

item.title: Lula slams US move to designate Brazil drug cartels as terrorist groups
item.description: Classification reignites tensions between Donald Trump and leftwing president

item.title: Universal Music declines takeover offer from Bill Ackman’s Pershing Square
item.description: Hedge fund billionaire argued moving record company’s listing from Amsterdam to New York would unlock value

item.title: US securities regulator seeks to end corporate disclosures on climate risk
item.description: SEC looking to overturn Biden-era requirement after finding it a ‘dramatic over-reach’ of authority

item.title: Citadel Securities posts record $4.3bn in trading revenues on Iran volatility
item.description: Market maker earns $1.9bn in first-quarter net income as oil prices and Treasury rates seesawed

item.title: Russian drone hits apartment building in Romania
item.description: Nato and Bucharest condemn ‘reckless’ escalation after two people injured in Galaţi city

item.title: Asda might help break Ocado’s supermarket stalemate
item.description: Tie-up suggests the online grocery delivery group is nearing its goal after it struggled to win new partners

item.title: Dell’s shares soar 35% as US computer maker taps into AI fervour
item.description: Rally is the latest sign of Wall Street’s exuberance over companies linked to artificial intelligence

item.title: SpaceX and the ‘enshittification’ of markets
item.description: The IPO of Elon Musk’s company is a big risk for the millions of investors who have put savings into passive investing

item.title: Directors’ Deals: Aviva chief Blanc adds to stake
item.description: Insurer’s share price has fallen by 8 per cent so far this year

item.title: Stockpickers: Diaceutics, Pets at Home, Autotrader Group
item.description: Our experts discuss which companies to buy, sell or hold this week

item.title: Kenyan court blocks US Ebola quarantine camp after protests
item.description: A doctors’ group in Kenya also protested about the use of their country as a ‘containment colony’

item.title: Meg O’Neill, the no-nonsense CEO steering BP through crisis
item.description: The ‘bullying’ scandal that led to chair Albert Manifold’s removal coincides with an attempted turnaround
